prompts: The Siberian Basin, also known as the West Siberian Plain, is a large geographical region in Russia characterized by flat terrain with some rolling hills. Its topography shows a gradual slope from its southwestern boundary towards the northeast, consisting mostly of lowlands, marshes, and river valleys. Contour lines in this area would illustrate subtle elevation changes, highlighting its predominantly flat landscape with elevation ranging approximately from 100 to 300 meters above sea level. It is bordered by the Ural Mountains to the west, the Central Siberian Plateau to the east, and stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Kazakhstan border in the south.
